description_long: This object is a tall, rectangular, column-like structure with a vertical orientation, resembling a scientific or geological core sample display. The main body is encased in a white or light-colored frame, with a central section filled with a compacted, earth-toned material that appears to be soil or sediment. Along one side, there is a clearly marked vertical measuring scale, labeled in centimeters from 0 to 90, which is printed in black on a white background. The surface of the central section is rough and uneven, indicating the natural texture of the material inside. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or decorative features, and the object appears to be intended for display or measurement purposes in an urban or scientific context.
description: A tall, rectangular obelisk with a white frame, filled with compacted earth or sediment and a vertical measuring scale marked in centimeters.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the obelisk, the earth-filled central section, and the measuring scale clearly visible on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the obelisk, with the measuring scale edge-on and the earth-filled section barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the earth-filled section and the measuring scale, but with slightly different lighting and surface details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the measuring scale edge-on, with the white frame and minimal visibility of the earth-filled section.
attribute_inscription: Vertical measuring scale in black numerals on a white background, running from 0 to 90 centimeters along one side; numerals are clear and legible.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple rectangular extension of the frame, flush with the sides and bottom, with no decorative features or steps; constructed from the same white material as the frame.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and squared off, matching the rectangular profile of the obelisk, with no unique features or decorations.
